cards.title: Plato's Cave
cards.body: Imagine being imprisoned in a cave, facing a wall where shadows are projected. This thought experiment by Plato explores the nature of reality and perception.

cards.title: Epicurus' Hedonism
cards.body: Epicurus believed that the key to happiness is living modestly and limiting desires, rather than pursuing excessive pleasure.

cards.title: The Veil of Ignorance
cards.body: Philosopher John Rawls proposed that we should design a just society from behind a 'veil of ignorance', without knowing our own social status or position.

cards.title: Moral Relativism
cards.body: Moral relativism is the idea that moral judgments are relative to the cultural or personal context, and there is no universal moral truth.

quizzes.question: Who wrote the Allegory of the Cave?
quizzes.explanation: Plato wrote the Allegory of the Cave to explore the nature of reality and perception.

quizzes.question: What is the main idea behind the Veil of Ignorance?
options: To design a just society without knowing one's social status
quizzes.explanation: The Veil of Ignorance is a thought experiment that aims to design a just society by removing personal biases and interests.
